content.text:
I'll create a complete Vite + React + Tailwind CSS analytics dashboard application for you. Let me start by analyzing the requirements and setting up the project.

## Analysis
This is a frontend-only analytics dashboard with:
- Time range and department filters
- Configurable external JSON endpoints
- Top 20 records retrieval
- Automatic chart type detection based on data
- No explicit database/backend requirement mentioned (will use fetch for external endpoints)

I'll use the `configuring-vite-environments` skill for the Vite setup since no backend services are needed.
Now I'll create the complete analytics dashboard application with all the required features.

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true
  },
  "include": ["src"],
  "references": [{ "path": "./tsconfig.node.json" }]
}

input.content:
# Analytics Dashboard

A modern, interactive analytics and reporting dashboard built with Vite, React, and Tailwind CSS. Features dynamic data visualization with automatic chart type detection, configurable external JSON endpoints, and comprehensive filtering capabilities.

## Features

- **Configurable Data Sources**: Add and manage external JSON endpoints directly from the dashboard
- **Automatic Chart Detection**: Intelligently determines the best visualization (bar, line, area, pie, or table) based on data structure
- **Time Range Filters**: Week, Month, Quarter, Year, and All Time views
- **Department Filters**: Filter analytics by specific departments or view all
- **Top 20 Records**: Automatically retrieves only the top 20 records from each endpoint
- **Real-time Updates**: Dynamic data fetching and visualization
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-friendly interface with Tailwind CSS
- **Interactive Charts**: Built with Recharts for rich, interactive visualizations

## Project Structure

```
/home/node/txai-projects/project/
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── ChartRenderer.tsx       # Chart visualization with auto-detection
│   │   ├── FilterPanel.tsx         # Time range and department filters
│   │   └── ConfigurationPanel.tsx  # Endpoint configuration interface
│   ├── types/
│   │   └── index.ts                # TypeScript interfaces and types
│   ├── utils/
│   │   └── dataFetcher.ts          # Data fetching and chart type detection
│   ├── App.tsx                     # Main application component
│   ├── main.tsx                    # Application entry point
│   └── index.css                   # Tailwind CSS styles
├── index.html
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── tsconfig.json
├── tailwind.config.js
└── postcss.config.js
```

## Installation

1. **Install dependencies:**
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

## Running the Application

### Development Mode

Start the Vite development server:

```bash
mkdir -p /home/node/txai-projects/project/.logs && npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 > /home/node/txai-projects/project/.logs/server.log 2>&1
```

The application will be available at `http://localhost:5173`

### Production Build

Build the application for production:

```bash
npm run build
```

Preview the production build:

```bash
npm run preview
```

## Technology Stack

- **Vite** - Fast build tool and dev server with HMR
- **React 18** - UI library with functional components and hooks
- **TypeScript** - Type-safe development
- **Tailwind CSS** - Utility-first CSS framework
- **Recharts** - Composable charting library for React
- **PostCSS & Autoprefixer** - CSS processing

## High Level Design

### Architecture

The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

1. **Data Layer** (`utils/dataFetcher.ts`):
   - Fetches data from external JSON endpoints
   - Limits results to top 20 records per endpoint
   - Automatically detects optimal chart type based on data structure

2. **Component Layer**:
   - `ConfigurationPanel`: Manages data source endpoints (add, enable/disable, delete)
   - `FilterPanel`: Controls time range and department filters
   - `ChartRenderer`: Renders visualizations based on detected chart type

3. **State Management**:
   - React hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`) for local state
   - Real-time data fetching triggered by endpoint or filter changes

### Chart Detection Algorithm

The dashboard analyzes data structure to automatically select the best visualization:

- **Table**: Complex data (6+ fields) or unknown structures
- **Pie Chart**: 1-3 data points with numeric values
- **Line/Area Chart**: Time-series data (detects date/time fields)
- **Bar Chart**: Categorical data with numeric values

## Usage Instructions

### Adding Data Endpoints

1. Click on "Data Source Configuration" to expand the panel
2. Enter an endpoint name and JSON URL
3. Click "Add Endpoint" to save
4. Toggle endpoints on/off using the checkboxes
5. Delete unwanted endpoints using the "Delete" button

### Applying Filters

- **Time Range**: Select Week, Month, Quarter, Year, or All Time
- **Department**: Choose a specific department or "All Departments"

### Sample Endpoints

The dashboard comes pre-configured with a sample endpoint. You can add your own JSON endpoints that return arrays of objects. Examples:

- `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users`
- `https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts`
- `https://api.github.com/users`

### Data Requirements

- Endpoints must return valid JSON
- Data should be an array of objects
- Only the first 20 records will be processed
- Objects should have consistent field structures

## Troubleshooting

### Charts not displaying
- Verify the endpoint URL is valid and returns JSON
- Check browser console for CORS or network errors
- Ensure the endpoint is enabled in the configuration panel

### Data not updating
- Refresh the page to clear any cached data
- Verify network connectivity to external endpoints
- Check that filters are not excluding all data

### CORS Issues
- External APIs must have CORS enabled
- Consider using CORS-enabled endpoints or proxy services
- Some public APIs may have rate limits

## Environment Variables

This project uses Vite's environment variable system:

- `VITE_BASE_PATH` - Base path for deployment (default: `/`)

## License

MIT License - Built by Leona, HCL Software's Vibe Coding Agent

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully created a complete **Analytics Dashboard** application with all the requested features. Here's what has been built:

## ✅ **Complete Feature Implementation**

### **1. Time Range Filters**
- Week, Month, Quarter, Year, and All Time options
- Implemented in `FilterPanel.tsx` with button-based selection

### **2. Department Filters**
- Dropdown to filter by specific department or view all
- Pre-configured with 5 departments (Sales, Engineering, Marketing, HR, Finance)

### **3. Configurable Dashboard Filters**
- Both filters can be changed directly from the dashboard
- Real-time updates when filters are changed

### **4. External JSON Endpoint Configuration**
- `ConfigurationPanel.tsx` allows adding/removing endpoints
- Enable/disable endpoints with checkboxes
- User-friendly interface for managing data sources

### **5. Top 20 Records Retrieval**
- `dataFetcher.ts` utility automatically limits results to 20 records
- Applied to all endpoint requests

### **6. Automatic Chart Type Detection**
- **Intelligent algorithm** in `dataFetcher.ts` analyzes data structure:
  - **Bar Chart**: Categorical data with numeric values
  - **Line Chart**: Time-series data (multiple numeric fields)
  - **Area Chart**: Time-series data (single numeric field)
  - **Pie Chart**: Small datasets (1-3 records) with numeric values
  - **Table**: Complex data or unknown structures

### **7. Visual Components**
- `ChartRenderer.tsx` renders 5 different visualization types
- Built with **Recharts** library for interactive, responsive charts
- Beautiful styling with **Tailwind CSS**

## 🚀 **Application Running**

Your application is now live at:
- **Local**: http://localhost:5173/app-a1c6336b4589506e8e6753333a075d83/
- **Network**: http://192.168.28.34:5173/app-a1c6336b4589506e8e6753333a075d83/

## 📂 **Project Structure**

```
/home/node/txai-projects/project/
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── ChartRenderer.tsx       # Auto-detecting chart visualization
│   │   ├── FilterPanel.tsx         # Time & department filters
│   │   └── ConfigurationPanel.tsx  # Endpoint management
│   ├── types/index.ts              # TypeScript definitions
│   ├── utils/dataFetcher.ts        # Data fetching & chart detection
│   ├── App.tsx                     # Main dashboard
│   ├── main.tsx                    # Entry point
│   └── index.css                   # Tailwind styles
├── README.md                       # Complete documentation
└── Configuration files (vite, tsconfig, tailwind, etc.)
```

## 🎯 **How to Use**

1. **Add Data Sources**: Click "Data Source Configuration" to add JSON endpoints
2. **Apply Filters**: Select time range and department from the filter panel
3. **View Analytics**: Charts automatically render with optimal visualization type
4. **Manage Sources**: Enable/disable or delete endpoints as needed

## 🌟 **Key Features**

- ✨ **Smart Visualization**: Automatically picks the best chart for your data
- 🎨 **Modern UI**: Gradient backgrounds, smooth transitions, hover effects
- 📱 **Responsive**: Works beautifully on mobile, tablet, and desktop
- ⚡ **Fast**: Built with Vite for lightning-fast HMR
- 🔧 **Configurable**: No code changes needed to add new data sources

The dashboard comes with a sample endpoint pre-configured. You can test it immediately or add your own JSON endpoints to visualize your data!
